target_population.description: Non-institutionalized adults age 18 or older residing in the United States who can complete an English-language online self-administered survey.
inclusion_criteria: Current residence in the United States or District of Columbia.
Able to complete the survey in English without assistance.
Provides informed consent before any ANX-Bench item is administered.
exclusion_criteria: Residence outside the United States or District of Columbia.
Unable to self-administer the English survey.
Duplicate, vendor-confirmed fraud, withdrawal of consent, breakoff without usable ANX item data, or preregistered quality-control exclusion.
coverage_limits: The frame does not cover adults who cannot access or complete online surveys in English.
Institutionalized adults, adults without stable panel reachability, and adults excluded by vendor fraud controls are outside the covered analytic frame.
Weighted estimates are calibrated descriptive estimates for the covered online adult population, not a full probability-sample estimate of all US adults.

sampling_frame.frame_description: A high-quality professional online panel with profile variables for demographic quota monitoring, duplicate detection, AI-exposure screening, and post-fielding calibration to external margins.
known_undercoverage: Adults not reachable through online panels.
Adults with low digital access or low survey-panel participation propensity.
sampling_frame.deduplication_rule: Vendor and study-level duplicate checks must remove repeated panel identifiers, device or fingerprint duplicates when available, and records flagged as confirmed fraud before weights are finalized.

recruitment_source.vendor_or_panel: Professional online panel vendor selected before fielding
recruitment_source.recruitment_mode: Panel invitation with quota monitoring and respondent-level eligibility screening before ANX-Bench item exposure.
recruitment_source.eligibility_screening: Screening confirms age, US residence, English self-administration ability, consent, and no known prior participation in Wave 8 pilot or bridge fielding when protected panel linkage is available.
recruitment_source.nonresponse_tracking: Vendor disposition counts must archive invitations, starts, screenouts, quota stops, breakoffs, completes, exclusions, and final analytic eligible cases.

quota_variables.margin_source: Current Population Survey or ACS adult margins available before weighting
CPS or vendor-calibrated adult panel benchmarks with nonbinary category monitored descriptively
ACS adult race and ethnicity margins
ACS or CPS adult education margins
CPS labor-force status margins where category mapping is stable
CPS occupation group margins for employed adults, with nonemployed monitored separately
No authoritative population margin; use same-wave distribution for sensitivity only
No authoritative population margin; use same-wave distribution for sensitivity only
No authoritative population margin frozen; monitor for relational-domain sensitivity and disclosure only
No authoritative population margin frozen; monitor for relational-domain sensitivity only
ACS or vendor-calibrated geography benchmarks when available before outcome inspection
No stable population margin frozen; monitor for safety-domain sensitivity and suppression only

planned_n_by_split.role: Independent full-domain bridge sample after preregistered exclusions.

construction_steps: Start from one base weight per eligible respondent because the planned panel source does not provide unequal selection probabilities for a probability sample.
Remove ineligible and preregistered excluded respondents before fitting final analytic weights; the usable eligible N must be at least 2000 for confirmatory bridge decisions.
Rake eligible respondents to external adult margins for age group, gender, race and ethnicity, education, and Census region.

Evaluate employment status, occupation group, and urbanicity as calibration variables when external margins map cleanly to collected categories; otherwise report them as monitored sensitivity variables.
Do not rake on AI exposure, AI-news exposure, relationship status, caregiving role, or cyber and infrastructure employment unless an external benchmark is frozen before outcome inspection.
Trim weights to the frozen lower and upper bounds, rescale to mean one, and archive weighted and unweighted diagnostics before outcome analysis.

survey_weight_mapping.mapping_rule: Copy respondent-level final_weight to survey_weight on every respondent-item row after trimming and mean-one rescaling.

diagnostics: Effective sample size overall and by planned split.
Weight minimum, maximum, mean, median, coefficient of variation, design effect, and percent trimmed.
Weighted and unweighted distributions for each quota variable.
Maximum absolute marginal deviation from each external raking target.
Sensitivity table comparing primary descriptive estimates with unweighted estimates.

trim_bounds.exception_rule: Weights outside 0.30 to 3.00 are trimmed before rescaling; wider bounds require a frozen addendum before outcome inspection and cannot be chosen based on ANX outcomes.

variance_design.primary_standard_error: Taylor linearization using final survey_weight for weighted descriptive estimates; psychometric model decisions remain governed by the analysis plan.
variance_design.software: R survey package, version locked in the Wave 8 analysis scripts before outcome inspection

subgroup_cells.suppression_rule: Suppress or collapse any age cell with fewer than 75 eligible respondents after exclusions.
Suppress any gender category with fewer than 50 eligible respondents; do not use sparse categories for confirmatory difference claims.
Suppress or combine race and ethnicity categories only according to the frozen codebook; report sparse cells as underpowered.
Suppress education subgroup estimates with fewer than 100 eligible respondents or unstable weight diagnostics.
Suppress regional estimates with fewer than 100 eligible respondents or effective sample size below 70.
Report AI exposure groups as sample-calibrated descriptive contrasts only because no authoritative population margin is frozen.
Do not report crossed occupation by AI exposure cells as benchmark subgroup claims unless a later plan freezes larger cell targets and variance treatment.
Report relational-domain sensitivity only when each displayed cell has at least 100 eligible respondents and passes disclosure review.
Suppress caregiver subgroup estimates with fewer than 100 eligible respondents or unstable weight diagnostics.
Suppress urbanicity estimates with fewer than 100 eligible respondents or effective sample size below 70.
Suppress safety-domain subgroup estimates with fewer than 75 eligible respondents and never report employer-specific or incident-specific cells.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Weighted and unweighted description of the Wave 8 eligible bridge sample and its covered online adult frame.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Sample description is required to interpret bridge evidence and does not imply probability-sample representativeness.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Calibrated descriptive estimates for covered English-language online US adults, labeled as nonprobability weighted estimates.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The recruitment source is a nonprobability online panel, so weighted estimates require explicit nonprobability limitations and cannot be described as nationally representative probability estimates.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: Prespecified single-variable subgroup descriptions when minimum cell sizes, suppression rules, and weight diagnostics are satisfied.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 8 is powered for bridge psychometrics, not confirmatory population subgroup difference testing.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No Wave 8 longitudinal trend, change, or repeated-cross-section claim is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: Wave 8 is an independent cross-domain bridge sample and is not frozen as a trend wave.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No event-window, model-release shock, or causal event-study claim is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The Wave 8 event registry is locked to no_event and the sampling plan contains no baseline or follow-up event design.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No causal effect claim is authorized from Wave 8.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The bridge wave is observational and contains no randomized or quasi-experimental exposure design for causal inference.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No clinical, diagnostic, treatment, screening, or individual-risk interpretation is authorized.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: ANX-Bench Wave 8 measures research constructs and has no clinical validation or diagnostic threshold.

claim_limits.allowed_scope: No respondent-level decision, ranking, eligibility, employment, clinical, policy action, full-domain score, cross-domain score, domain-combined score, or overall ANX score may use Wave 8 responses.
claim_limits.limit_rationale: The benchmark is designed for aggregate research evidence, not decisions about individual respondents, and Wave 8 is a frozen candidate bridge packet with aggregate_scoring_permitted set to false.
